tagline: Quiet week on GitHub. 170 commits across the autoresearch universe. No releases.
editionNote: 170 commits spread across 10 repos, mostly autoresearch variants. No releases, no merged PRs, no fires to report.

articles.headline: autoresearch stuck in the issues queue
articles.deck: Five open issues outline the next frontier: licensing questions, pluggable optimization tracks, and a pivot toward data-centric research.
articles.body: @karpathy has filed five substantial issues against autoresearch this week, none yet merged. The most ambitious: Auto-Research Tracks, which proposes pluggable idle optimization across harness, skills, memory, and model selection. A separate pitch for data-centric autoresearch suggests the project may be at an inflection point. Pending work also includes a JudgeModel layer for snapshot-diff scoring and a call to expand the harness generator into the pattern/memory/security design space. Licensing remains an open question.
articles.illustrationPrompt: interconnected nodes and pathways glowing in darkness, suggesting autonomous agents exploring multiple optimization tracks simultaneously

articles.headline: autoresearch-cpu accumulates commits
articles.deck: The CPU-bound variant of autoresearch picked up 17 commits this week but remains undocumented in the public record.
articles.body: @karpathy's CPU adaptation saw steady churn. No releases, no PRs, no issues filed. The work is opaque from the outside.
articles.illustrationPrompt: silicon pathways and logic gates arranged in a glowing lattice, minimal and abstract

articles.headline: EvolvedAI keeps the lights on
articles.deck: The social simulation fork of autoresearch registered 17 commits but no user-facing activity.
articles.body: @karpathy committed to the prompt-only social simulation built from autoresearch, but nothing shipped or broke this week.
articles.illustrationPrompt: miniature civilization with buildings and figures in warm light, abstract and bird's-eye view

articles.headline: autokernel5090 waits for the RTX 5090
articles.deck: GPU-specific kernel work accumulated 17 commits with no visible release or PR activity.
articles.body: @karpathy is preparing infrastructure for the RTX 5090. The commits landed but stayed under wraps. Likely not production-ready yet.
articles.illustrationPrompt: high-end graphics processor chip glowing with internal circuitry and heat signatures

articles.headline: TrainRTX5070 training pipeline moves quietly
articles.deck: Continual learning setup for RTX 5070 saw 17 commits this week with no releases or issues filed.
articles.body: @karpathy's RTX 5070 training pipeline accumulated work in silence. No public signals yet on what the commits contain or when they ship.
articles.illustrationPrompt: modern graphics card under studio lighting with neural network visualizations flowing across its surface

closingNote: 170 commits. Zero releases. Patience is a strategy.
